summary.intent: Identify and fix performance bottlenecks in the gossamer codebase — specifically missing DB indexes, redundant git calls, N+1 queries, and slow startup — with the goal of speeding up plugin startup and SessionDetail tab open time.
summary.outcome: 15 discrete performance fixes were implemented and typechecked clean across packages/core, src/server.ts, and packages/vscode: dedup query scoping, git log --grep replacement, config caching, batch git log for checkpoints, flattened branch-log N+1, single /api/sessions response, parallelized repo init, watermark-based FTS sync, PRAGMA-gated ALTER TABLE, tightened VS Code health poll, and scoped WebSocket broadcast with a delta log-events endpoint.

repo: gossamer uses a per-repo SQLite pattern: each repo gets its own Prisma DB, initialized via pushSchema on startup, with schema applied via raw SQL from a generated schema.sql artifact.
pushSchema runs every schema statement with IF NOT EXISTS but ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN lacks that guard in SQLite, so duplicate-column failures were swallowed per-repo on every cold start.

The FTS table (LogContentFts) is a manual shadow table synced via INSERT ... WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT rowid FROM LogContentFts) rather than FTS5 triggers, making every cold-start sync an O(N×M) anti-join.
The gitlog npm package was imported only for findCommitForCheckpoint and has been fully replaced by a git log --grep shell call; the package remains in package.json but is no longer used.
The VS Code extension starts the gossamer HTTP server as a child process and polls /health to detect readiness; the poll interval directly affects perceived startup latency.
WebSocket broadcast (sessions_updated) was global — any change in any repo triggered all connected webviews to refetch their full session data.
indexFullJsonlContent loaded ALL uuid and messageId values from the entire LogEvent table on every call as a dedup set, regardless of how many new events were being processed.

code.finding: Loaded entire uuid+messageId sets from LogEvent DB on every indexing call; fixed by scoping WHERE uuid/messageId IN (keys from file), chunked at 500.
findCommitForCheckpoint fetched up to 10k commits into JS memory and linear-scanned for a trailer match; replaced with git log -1 --grep='^Entire-Checkpoint: <id>$'.
readConfig() re-read and JSON.parsed ~/.gossamer/config.json on every call; fixed with mtimeMs-keyed cache; writeConfig updates cache in place.
/api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints forked one git log process per checkpoint × candidate dir; replaced with single git log --no-walk --format=%H%x00%s <oid1> <oid2>... per repo.
/api/branch-log ran Promise.all([sessions, cpMeta]) + per-session findFirst in a serial loop; flattened to two findMany calls plus one chunked SQL join for hasEvents check.
logEvent.findMany({ distinct: ['sessionId'] }) scanned entire LogEvent table to find sessions without metadata — almost always a no-op; removed.
sessionParent.findMany({}) was unbounded — fetched all parent-child rows on every /api/sessions call; now scoped to IDs in the current page.
Four ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN statements ran per repo on every cold start even when columns already existed; now gated behind PRAGMA table_info probe.
initRepoDb was called in a serial for...await loop; changed to Promise.all so N repos initialize concurrently.
syncLogContentFts used WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT rowid FROM LogContentFts) anti-join on every cold start; replaced with MAX(rowid) watermark check, anti-join only on empty FTS table.
waitForServer polled /health every 500ms with no early fast path; changed to 75ms for first 2s then 500ms, with 200ms fetch timeout.

fetchSessions(false) and fetchSessions(true) called in parallel on every refresh, causing the server to aggregate everything twice; replaced with single fetchAllSessions() that returns archived flag per row.
Re-fetched full session + all log events on every sessions_updated WebSocket tick from any repo; now filters by repoDbPath and uses ?sinceId= delta endpoint to append only new events.

workflow: tsc --noEmit was used as the primary correctness gate after each change across three tsconfigs: packages/core, root (server), and packages/vscode/tsconfig.extension.json.
Vite build of the webview was used as a type-check equivalent for the React/tsx files since they don't have a standalone tsc config.
Performance fixes were sequenced by direct impact on the two user-visible flows (startup, tab open) rather than by ease of implementation.

The Prisma pushSchema pattern (IF NOT EXISTS SQL statements run on every server boot) means new indexes added to schema.prisma are automatically applied to existing per-repo DBs on next start — no explicit migration step needed.

friction: App startup still took 10+ seconds after the first round of fixes, requiring a second audit pass specifically targeting cold-start serialization.
User had to ask whether new DB indexes had been migrated — audit report mentioned adding indexes but none were actually added to schema.prisma in this session, creating ambiguity.
The webview has no standalone tsc config, requiring a full Vite build to catch type errors in React components — slower feedback loop than tsc --noEmit.
Multiple typecheck passes were needed throughout the session as changes in server.ts and the webview API shape rippled into dependent files.
open_items: @@index([parentToolUseId]) on LogEvent still not added to schema.prisma — explicitly deferred; impacts shadow indexer's indexTaskRelationships which does a full scan per branch.
createMany batching for LogContent/LogUsage/LogHookProgress inserts during indexing (N+1 inserts per event) — deferred; biggest remaining win for first-time index of long sessions.
gitlog npm package is still in package.json but no longer imported anywhere after gitUtils.ts fix — should be removed from dependencies.

WebSocket SessionDetail delta work was in progress at end of transcript (tasks created, server-side sinceId endpoint added, webview api.ts updated) but final wiring of ActiveSessions.tsx subscribeToUpdates scope arg was the last edit — needs end-to-end verification.
git ls-tree parsed twice per shadow pass (indexer.ts:819 and 1146) — identified but not fixed.
Per-row git blame author backfill (indexer.ts:945-957, 1059-1071) — identified but not fixed; sequential updates per row.

setupLogContentFts + syncLogContentFts triggered after every shadow/live indexing pass regardless of whether new rows were inserted — identified but only partially addressed (cold-start path fixed; per-indexing-pass trigger not changed).
livePoller polling every 3s across all repos with fs.stat per directory — identified but not addressed; suggested raising to 10-15s.
gitConfigGet shells out two git config processes per cache miss — identified but not fixed.
